content.text:
Looking at the rendering:

- Referent 2 (the victim) is a `SomethingRef`, but the gloss says "living being"—rendered via LIVE(2) before time 3, so life prior to the act is encoded.
- DO(agent=1, undergoer=2) — an act by X upon Y.
- BEFORE(time3, LIVE(2)) — Y was alive.
- BECAUSE(DO(1,2), DIE(2)) — the act causes Y to die: the criterial causal core.
- A death-kind event HAPPENs at time 3, with the inlined death sub-explication (someone dies, afterwards does not live).

The causing-to-die content — prior life, agent's act, DIE as effect via BECAUSE, death event — is all present. Directness/intent correctly noted as unrendered pragmatic shadings. The gloss is genus–differentia (causing death by act upon a living being), sense-fixed, non-circular. Self-flag "faithful" is honest. No sense collision.
